title: Medium throughput protein expression and purification for protein design using heat lysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fab91","text":"This protocols describes medium throughput protein expression in E. coli using heat-lysis as a built in purification step. It is intended for preparation of heat-stable de novo designed proteins for screening in vitro.1-3 It provides a small but relatively pure lysate sample of the protein within 2 working days.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":65,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":169,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":209,"length":8},{"style":"sup","offset":218,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7lrk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"clgqu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qgl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c7158","text":"Remember to always keep track of the samples when transferring between plate types.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"firbn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fl7qh","text":"Gene of interest should be in a pET system or another IPTG inducible expression vector.4","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":87,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2lljp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fq890","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ptir","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Before starting: Thaw competent E. coli BL21 On ice Thaw plasmid DNA Room temperature Cool a sterile 96-well plate On ice Preheat the water bath 42 °C
Add 10 µL E. coli BL21 per well in the 96-well plate.

Add 1 µL plasmid DNA solution to the bacteria. Stir with pipette tip to connect droplets, do not pipette up and down. Alternatively, spin the plate after adding the DNA to make sure that cells and DNA come into contact.
Incubate the plate 00:15:00 On ice .
Heat-shock bacteria in water bath by holding the plate half submerged in the water 42 °C 00:00:30 . Put directly back on ice afterwards.
Add 50 µL sterile LB medium to each well.
Incubate transformation mixtures 400 rpm, 37 °C, 1 hour .
Prepare autoinduction medium with appropriate antibiotic in a sterile reservoir.
Add 2 mL autoinduction medium to each well in 4 sterile 24-well plates.
Cover plates with breathable film.
Incubate cultures 400 rpm, 37 °C, Overnight .
Add 90 µL LB per well in a 96-well plate. Should be plate reader compatible.
Add 10 µL of culture per well. Make sure to resuspend if pelleted.

Measure OD600 in the plate reader. Remember to include a couple of wells with just LB as blank controls. OD of the culture will then be 10x this number. This step is qualitative and meant as a quick check of the growth. The values vary between experiments.
Take a 15 µl sample of each production from diluted 96-well plate samples and save for SDS-PAGE analysis.
Pellet bacteria in the 24-well plate 2500 x g, Room temperature, 15 minutes
Remove the supernatant. Freeze 24-well plate or proceed to lysis.
Preheat Thermomixer 95 °C

Resuspend the pellets in 500 µL lysis buffer and move to a heat-stable 96-well plate. If the lysate is very viscous you can increase the volume, but this will decrease the final protein concentration.
Heat-lyse the bacteria 95 °C 00:15:00 .
Transfer the lysate to a 96-well filter plate stacked on a collection plate (96 deep-well plate).
Centrifuge 1500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . If the liquid in some wells has not gone fully through, resuspend with pipette and centrifuge again.
Take 15 µl samples from cleared lysates for SDS-PAGE.
Estimate protein concentration in the lysates using the Bicinchoninic Acid (BCA) Method.
Optional to ease filtration: Centrifuge 3000 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes and take only the supernatant to the next step.
Decide on your lysis protocol. This will depend on the stability of your proteins of interest.

Resuspend pelleted bateria in 300 µl of B-PER Complete BacterialProtein Extraction Reagent (optinally supplemented with 20 mM PMSF to avoid degradation by proteases in the lysate). The volume of B-PER needed will depend on the size of the pellet. It might be possible to use less than 300 µl, but with too little the lysate can become very viscous.
Incubate gently shaking 00:15:00 Room temperature
Centrifuge to separate soluble protein from insoluble cell debris. 2500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes, or as high speed as your centrifuge and plates can take Keep the supernatant.
Take SDS-PAGE samples from cleared lysates.
Estimate protein concentration in the lysates using the Bicinchoninic Acid (BCA) Method.
Prepare 200 mL purification buffer (20 mM imidazole) and 60 mL elution buffer (500 mM imidazole). Open the plate according to intrsuctions and place it on a collection plate (96-deepwell).
Centrifuge the plate to remove the storage solution from the resin 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes
Add 500 μL deionized water/well 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes .
Add 500 μL purification buffer/well and mix briefly to equilibrate the resin, centrifuge 500 rpm, 4 °C, 2 minutes . Repeat once.
Apply filtered lysate to the wells, mix briefly, and incubate 00:03:00
Remove the flow through by centrifuging 100 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes, or until the wells are empty
Add 500 μL purification buffer/well and mix briefly to wash out unbound sample, centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes Repeat once.

Add 200 μL of elution buffer/well and mix 00:01:00 . Change collection plate and centrifuge the plates 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes and collect the fractions. Repeat twice. If required, change collection plate between each elution (to prevent unnecessary dilution of thetarget protein).
Prepare 200 mL binding buffer (20 mM imidazole) and 60 mL elution buffer (500 mM imidazole). Open the plate according to instructions and place it on top of a collection plate (96 deep-well).
Centrifuge the plate to remove the storage solution from the resin 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes
Add 500 μL deionized water/well 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes .
Add 500 μL binding buffer/well and mix briefly to equilibrate the resin, centrifuge 500 rpm, 4 °C, 2 minutes . Repeat once.
Apply filtered lysate to the wells, mix briefly, and incubate 00:03:00
Remove the flow-through by centrifuging 100 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes, or until the wells are empty
Add 500 μL binding buffer/well and mix briefly to wash out unbound sample, centrifuge 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes Repeat once.

Add 200 μL of elution buffer/well and mix for 00:01:00 . Change collection plate and centrifuge the plates 500 x g, 4 °C, 2 minutes and collect the fractions. Repeat twice. If required, change collection plate between each elution (to prevent unnecessary dilution of thetarget protein).
creator: Ida Kjærsgaard Grene
